description_long: This object is a curved sword with a single-edged blade, reminiscent of a sabre or cutlass, accompanied by a matching sheath. The blade is slightly curved and tapers to a rounded point, with a metallic sheen indicating a steel or iron composition. The handle features a prominent knuckle guard, likely made of brass or a similar alloy, and is ergonomically shaped for a secure grip. The sheath is dark, possibly leather-wrapped, with metallic fittings at both ends for reinforcement and decorative effect. The overall design suggests a utilitarian yet elegant weapon, suitable for ceremonial or practical use. The materials and construction indicate durability and a balance between function and style.
description: A curved, single-edged sword with a brass knuckle guard and a dark, metal-tipped sheath. The blade is metallic and the handle is ergonomically designed for grip.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved blade and sheath, highlighting the brass guard and metal sheath fittings.
description_view_1: Edge-on view showing the thin profile of both the blade and sheath, with minimal visible detail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again displaying the full curvature of the blade and sheath, with the guard and fittings visible from the other side.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite direction, showing the narrowness of the blade and sheath.
attribute_handle_design: curved handle with brass knuckle guard, smooth grip, possibly wood or leather-wrapped
